properties.name: Oracle of Mount Olympus
properties.summary: Seat of the twelve gods; pilgrims climb here to receive prophecy from the priestesses of Zeus.

properties.name: Sunken Halls of Atlantis
properties.summary: A kingdom swallowed by the sea in a single night, said to lie beneath the Atlantic shelf.

properties.name: Parthenon of Athena
properties.summary: The great temple atop the Acropolis, dedicated to Athena the goddess of wisdom, standing in gleaming marble above the city of Athens.

properties.name: Palace of Knossos
properties.summary: The legendary labyrinthine palace of King Minos, deep within which the Minotaur waits for its next tribute of Athenian youth.

properties.name: Ruins of Troy
properties.summary: The scorched walls of Ilion, sacked after ten years of siege. Only ash and legend remain where Helen once walked.

properties.name: Temple of Karnak
properties.summary: The vast complex of Amun-Ra, where the Nile priests maintained the balance of Ma'at and pharaohs bargained with the gods for eternal life.

properties.name: Great Pyramid of Osiris
properties.summary: Built as a gateway between the living and the dead, aligned to the stars of Orion so the pharaoh's soul might ascend to Osiris.

properties.name: Underworld Gate of Anubis
properties.summary: A desert shrine where Anubis is said to weigh the hearts of the dead against a feather. Those found heavy vanish into darkness.

properties.name: Valhalla — Hall of the Slain
properties.summary: Odin's great mead-hall where the bravest warriors feast until Ragnarok. The Valkyries ride at dusk to collect the worthy dead.

properties.name: Yggdrasil — The World Tree
properties.summary: The immense ash tree whose roots reach into all nine worlds. Odin hung himself from its branches for nine days to discover the runes.

properties.name: Ruins of Utgard
properties.summary: The fortress of the frost giants at the edge of the world, where Thor was humbled by illusions and the cat he could not lift was the Midgard Serpent.

properties.name: Temple of Brahma, Pushkar
properties.summary: One of the few temples dedicated to Brahma the Creator, built beside the sacred lake where a lotus fell from his hand and the world bloomed.

properties.name: Sunken City of Dwarka
properties.summary: The golden city of Krishna, swallowed by the sea the moment he departed the world. Divers still find its towers beneath the waves.

properties.name: Lanka — Ravana's Fortress
properties.summary: The island stronghold of the demon king Ravana, built of gold and terror, from which Rama and Hanuman's army came to rescue Sita.

properties.name: Mount Meru — Cosmic Axis
properties.summary: The sacred mountain at the center of the universe, home to the gods. Its peak pierces the heavens and its roots anchor the earth.

properties.name: Village of the Pandavas
properties.summary: The humble settlement where the five Pandava princes lived in exile, their fate tied to the great war described in the Mahabharata.

properties.name: Ruins of Persepolis
properties.summary: The ceremonial capital of the Achaemenid empire, built to honor Ahura Mazda. Burned by Alexander, its ghosts still walk the Hall of a Hundred Columns.

properties.name: Tower of Silence
properties.summary: A Zoroastrian dakhma where the dead are offered to the sun and the vultures, cleansing the body so the soul may ascend to Ahura Mazda's light.

properties.name: Palace of Zahhak the Serpent King
properties.summary: The accursed palace of the tyrant Zahhak, from whose shoulders serpents grew and demanded a daily meal of human brains.

properties.name: Jade Emperor's Temple, Chengdu
properties.summary: A celestial temple where the Jade Emperor receives tribute from all the gods of heaven. His court decides the fate of every living soul.

properties.name: Dragon Gate — Yellow River
properties.summary: The mythical waterfall where carp leap against the current. Any fish that makes it through transforms into a dragon. The bones of ten thousand failures line the riverbed.

properties.name: Mount Kunlun — Home of Xi Wangmu
properties.summary: The heavenly mountain home of the Queen Mother of the West, whose peach garden grows the fruit of immortality, ripening only once every three thousand years.

properties.name: Forbidden Celestial Palace, Beijing
properties.summary: The earthly reflection of the Jade Emperor's heavenly court, where the Son of Heaven ruled as intermediary between the mortal world and the divine.

properties.name: Village of the Eight Immortals
properties.summary: A misty mountain hamlet where the Eight Immortals are said to gather, each carrying their magical treasure before crossing the sea.

properties.name: Izumo Grand Shrine
properties.summary: The oldest and most sacred shrine in Japan, dedicated to Okuninushi. Every October all eight million kami gather here to decide the fates and marriages of mortals.

properties.name: Aokigahara — Gate to Yomi
properties.summary: The dense forest at the foot of Mount Fuji said to muffle all sound and compass needles. Ancient texts claim it grows over a crack leading to Yomi, the underworld.

properties.name: Ryūgū-jō — Dragon Palace
properties.summary: The underwater palace of Ryūjin, the dragon god of the sea, whose tidal jewels control the rise and fall of the ocean.

properties.name: Mount Fuji Shrine — Gateway to Heaven
properties.summary: The sacred peak of Fujisan, where the goddess Konohanasakuya-hime resides. Pilgrims climb through the night to greet the gods at the summit sunrise.

properties.name: Village of the Tengu
properties.summary: A remote mountain settlement where the long-nosed Tengu spirits are said to train wandering warriors in secret arts, then vanish with the morning mist.

properties.name: Chichen Itza — Temple of Kukulcan
properties.summary: The pyramid of the feathered serpent god. At each equinox a shadow snake descends its staircase, marking the moment the god returns to earth.

properties.name: Tikal — City of the Jaguar Lords
properties.summary: A once-great Maya city swallowed by jungle. Its pyramids pierce the canopy like stone fingers reaching for the sky, and jaguar priests still walk its plazas at night.

properties.name: Palenque — Palace of the Sun Shield
properties.summary: Tomb of Pakal the Great, whose sarcophagus lid shows him descending into Xibalba, the Maya underworld, on a world-tree throne.

properties.name: Uxmal — House of the Governor
properties.summary: Built by a dwarf who hatched from an egg and constructed the pyramid in a single night on the orders of the rain god Chaac.

properties.name: Xibalba — Entrance to the Underworld
properties.summary: The Hero Twins entered here through a cave to defeat the lords of death in a ball game. The howling and the cold that rise from these depths are unmistakable.

properties.name: Machu Picchu — City in the Clouds
properties.summary: An Inca citadel built so high in the Andes that the clouds pass through its streets. Raised in honor of Inti the sun god, its Intihuatana stone tethers the sun itself.

properties.name: Tiwanaku — Gateway of the Sun
properties.summary: The monolithic gateway carved with the image of Viracocha, creator of all things, who wept golden tears and fashioned humanity from clay at this very spot.

properties.name: Cuzco — Navel of the World
properties.summary: The sacred capital of the Inca, laid out in the shape of a puma. The Temple of the Sun at its heart was sheathed in gold to dazzle the eye of Inti.

properties.name: Island of the Sun, Lake Titicaca
properties.summary: The birthplace of the Inca creation myth, where Inti the sun god placed the first Inca, Manco Capac, and commanded him to found a civilization worthy of the light.

properties.name: Village of Viracocha
properties.summary: A remote Andean settlement said to be the last place Viracocha the creator god walked among humans before disappearing into the Pacific Ocean.

properties.name: Bear Butte — Sacred Mountain of the Lakota
properties.summary: The mountain where Sweet Medicine received the four sacred arrows from Maheo the All-Father, gifts that gave the Cheyenne their laws and their purpose.

properties.name: Cahokia Mounds — City of the Sun People
properties.summary: The greatest city north of Mexico before it was abandoned. Its Woodhenge aligned to the solstice, and its mounds are said to hum at midnight with ancestral voices.

properties.name: Mesa Verde — Cliff Palace
properties.summary: An Ancestral Puebloan city carved into the canyon cliff face, abandoned in a single generation. The kivas still hold the prayers of those who danced for rain.

properties.name: Serpent Mound — Ohio
properties.summary: A quarter-mile earthwork in the shape of an uncoiling serpent, aligned to the summer solstice sunset. No one is buried here. It was built only to be seen from the sky.

properties.name: Village of the Thunderbird
properties.summary: A Pacific Northwest settlement where the great Thunderbird is said to nest in the mountains above, its wingbeats making thunder and its eyes flashing lightning.
